steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the No Name Series' approach to publishing — books released anonymously, with authors' identities intentionally concealed. The word must fit that context of deliberate secrecy.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'preliminary': Means preparatory or introductory. The series wasn't a warm-up to something else; it was a complete publishing strategy. ✗
  • Choice B — 'clandestine': Means kept secret or concealed. This directly matches an approach built around anonymity and hidden authorship. ✓
  • Choice C — 'unequivocal': Means clear and leaving no doubt. This is the opposite of a secretive, identity-concealing approach. ✗
  • Choice D — 'inexplicable': Means impossible to explain. The approach is not baffling — it's a deliberate choice, not a mystery without a reason. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
